title: bonania domingensis
intro: Bonania domingensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae, native to the Caribbean, and is commonly known as the Hispaniolan poison ivy due to its similar appearance and irritating properties to poison ivy. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to reduce pain, swelling, and infection in various health issues. The health benefits of Bonania domingensis include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. Herbal preparations of Bonania domingensis can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and topical creams, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential to cause skin irritation in some individuals.
